title: Ceph CRUSH rule and failure domain consistency verification
dataSources: CRUSH map dumps indexed as `ceph:status` or dedicated `ceph:crush` sourcetype
app: Scripted input: `ceph osd crush rule dump` / `ceph osd tree` JSON
spl:
index=storage sourcetype="ceph:status" earliest=-24h
| search crush OR rule
| eval rule=coalesce(crush_rule_name, rule_name)
| eval domain=coalesce(failure_domain, device_class, host_bucket)
| stats values(domain) as domains dc(domain) as domain_count by rule, cluster_name
| where domain_count < 2 AND match(rule, "replicated|erasure")
| table cluster_name, rule, domains
description: Pools whose CRUSH rules do not enforce distinct failure domains risk simultaneous data loss when a rack or chassis fails. Automated diffing catches human error after hardware refreshes.
value: Supports data durability commitments and audit evidence for regulated object stores.
implementation: Nightly JSON snapshot; use `diff` saved to Splunk via scripted alert or store hash in KV for drift detection. Document allowed exceptions in a lookup.
knownFalsePositives: Short spikes during approved changes, maintenance windows, or known batch jobs can match the rule; confirm against the vendor console and change calendar.
grandmaExplanation: We help you catch when data placement rules no longer match your rack or site layout, before a single failure takes more data than you planned.
